title: Infinite Loops ♾️
description: Learn to avoid the most common programming trap.

slides.title: The Trap 🪤
slides.content:
We've mentioned it before, but the **Infinite Loop** is the most common bug beginners make.

It occurs when your loop condition is NEVER able to evaluate to `false`.

slides.title: When is it bad? 💥
slides.content:
If you accidentally write an infinite loop that prints `"Hello"`, your computer will try to print `"Hello"` millions of times a second.

Your program will freeze, your fans will spin up, and eventually, the app will crash because it runs out of memory (RAM).

slides.title: Can you spot the infinite loop? 🕵️‍♂️
slides.content: Why will this loop run forever?

quizData.question: Why does the code snippet above cause an infinite loop?

options.text: Because the `battery--` code is commented out, so the battery is forever 100.
Because it should be a for loop.

quizData.explanation: Since `battery--` was placed inside a comment (`//`), it never executes. `battery` stays at 100 forever, and `100 > 0` is always true.

slides.title: Are they ever good? 🎮
slides.content:
Sometimes you **want** an infinite loop!

For example, the engine running a 3D Video Game uses an infinite loop under the hood. It constantly draws a new frame to the screen 60 times a second, forever, until the user clicks "Quit Game" (which triggers a `break;` command to close the loop!).
